document: REDACTED HR Document: [NAME] (Employee ID: [EMPLOYEE_ID]) has been terminated effective 15-March-2026. His manager Rajesh Verma approved the decision. Reason: repeated policy violations. [NAME]'s final settlement of Rs. 3,45,000 will be credited to his HDFC account. Exit interview notes mention that the employee was diagnosed with bipolar disorder last year. Contact HR at hr@acmecorp.com for queries.
expected_redacted: [NAME] (Employee ID: [EMPLOYEE_ID]) has been terminated effective 15-March-2026. His manager [NAME] approved the decision. Reason: repeated policy violations. [NAME]'s final settlement of [SALARY] will be credited to his [ORGANIZATION] account. Exit interview notes mention that the employee was diagnosed with [MEDICAL_CONDITION] last year. Contact HR at [EMAIL] for queries.

expected_findings.recommended_action: Manager name should have been redacted — it links to the terminated employee's identity
Financial settlement amount must be redacted in shared documents
Sensitive health data left unredacted — this is a critical compliance failure
Corporate email reveals the organization — consider redacting in sensitive contexts

document: Patient Discharge Summary (REDACTED): Patient [NAME], age [AGE], was discharged from [ORGANIZATION] on 28-Feb-2026. Attending physician: Dr. Sunita Rao. Diagnosis: Type 2 diabetes with peripheral neuropathy. Prescribed medications: Insulin Glargine 20 units, Gabapentin 300mg. Patient's daughter Kavya (phone: 080-4555-7721) is the primary caregiver. expected_redacted: Patient [NAME], age [AGE], was discharged from [ORGANIZATION] on 28-Feb-2026. Attending physician: [NAME]. Diagnosis: [MEDICAL_CONDITION]. Prescribed medications: [MEDICATION], [MEDICATION]. Patient's daughter [NAME] (phone: [PHONE]) is the primary caregiver. Follow-up at the [LOCATION] branch in 2 weeks. Insurance ID: [EMPLOYEE_ID].

expected_findings.recommended_action: Physician name should be redacted — links to specific patient case
Diagnosis left completely unredacted — critical HIPAA violation
Family member name left exposed — identifies the patient indirectly
Phone number of caregiver completely unredacted

document: REDACTED Legal Memo — Workplace Harassment Case #WH-2026-044: Complainant: [NAME] (Dept: Marketing). Respondent: Vikram Sinha, Senior VP Operations. expected_redacted: REDACTED Legal Memo — Workplace Harassment Case #[EMPLOYEE_ID]: Complainant: [NAME] (Dept: Marketing). Respondent: [NAME], Senior VP Operations. The complainant has been on medical leave since January for [MEDICAL_CONDITION]. [NAME] resides at [ADDRESS].

expected_findings.recommended_action: Respondent name in harassment case must be redacted — critical failure under POSH confidentiality requirements
Witness names must be protected under POSH Act confidentiality provisions
Health condition linked to harassment case left exposed — sensitive health data violation
Full residential address of complainant left unredacted — enables physical identification

document: Insurance Claim (REDACTED): Claim ref [EMPLOYEE_ID]. Policyholder: [NAME], Aadhaar: [SSN]. Claim for: hospitalization at Fortis Hospital, Bangalore, from 10-15 Dec 2025. Diagnosis: acute liver failure secondary to chronic alcohol dependency. Total bill: Rs. 8,72,000. Nominee: wife Anita Kapoor, phone 99800-45612. Policy number: LIC-HLTH-2022-88412. Pre-existing condition: hypertension (disclosed at enrollment).

ground_truth.value: acute liver failure secondary to chronic alcohol dependency

expected_redacted: Insurance Claim (REDACTED): Claim ref [EMPLOYEE_ID]. Policyholder: [NAME], Aadhaar: [SSN]. Claim for: hospitalization at [ORGANIZATION], from 10-15 Dec 2025. Diagnosis: [MEDICAL_CONDITION]. Total bill: [SALARY]. Nominee: wife [NAME], phone [PHONE]. Policy number: [EMPLOYEE_ID]. Pre-existing condition: [MEDICAL_CONDITION] (disclosed at enrollment).
compliance_context: DPDP Act 2023, IRDAI regulations, Insurance Act 1938

expected_findings.value: acute liver failure secondary to chronic alcohol dependency
expected_findings.recommended_action: Highly sensitive diagnosis left fully exposed — alcohol dependency is stigmatizing medical data
Nominee/spouse name left unredacted — identifies the policyholder indirectly
Phone number of nominee completely exposed
Full policy number left unredacted — can be used to access complete claim history

document: School Records (REDACTED): Student [NAME], Class 8, Section B, Roll No. 23. Parent: [NAME], contact: [PHONE]. The student has been diagnosed with ADHD and is currently on Methylphenidate 10mg (administered by school nurse). Academic performance: below average. The student has been diagnosed with [MEDICAL_CONDITION] and is currently on [MEDICATION] (administered by school nurse). Academic performance: below average. compliance_context: DPDP Act 2023 Section 9(1), POCSO Act, Right to Education Act

expected_findings.recommended_action: Child's medical diagnosis left exposed — children's sensitive data requires highest protection under DPDP
Controlled substance prescribed to a minor left unredacted — critical failure for children's data protection
Counselor name reveals the child is receiving mental health treatment
Partial Aadhaar number of a minor left exposed — even partial biometric IDs of children must be fully redacted
